pi_items.metadata_value: Dev = A + B*sin(H) + C*cos(H) + D*sin(2H) + E*cos(2H)
pi_items.verified_value: Dev = A + B*sin(H) + C*cos(H) + D*sin(2H) + E*cos(2H)
pi_items.source: Academic literature on compass deviation (https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/25725084.2019.1569336); Handbook of Magnetic Compass Adjustment (NGA)

pi_items.key: Coefficients A, B, C, D, E
pi_items.verified_value: Valid coefficients that produce realistic deviation values ranging from -1.8° to +6.3° across all 360° headings
pi_items.source: Mathematical calculation using standard Admiralty formula

pi_items.metadata_value: 24 rows for headings 000 to 345 in 15-degree steps with deviations rounded to 1 decimal place
pi_items.verified_value: Correct structure with 24 entries (360°/15° = 24 points); calculated deviations are mathematically sound
pi_items.source: Calculation of full deviation table using provided coefficients

pi_items.verified_value: Coordinates are accurate for the Solent area; between Portsmouth and Southampton Water in southern England
pi_items.source: Admiralty Charts and GPS coordinate mapping (https://www.gpsnauticalcharts.com/)

pi_items.metadata_value: Using Bridge Command open-source maritime simulator
pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command is a real, actively maintained open-source 3D maritime simulator for training navigation and radar skills
pi_items.source: Official Bridge Command website (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/); GitHub repository (https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc)

pi_items.metadata_value: Instructor guide references SOLAS Chapter V and magnetic compass requirements
pi_items.verified_value: SOLAS Chapter V Regulation 19 establishes mandatory magnetic compass requirements for all vessels (with exceptions)
Current magnetic variation in Solent region (2024-2026) is +0.68°E to +0.77°E (EASTERLY, not westerly)
pi_items.source: World Magnetic Model data; British Geological Survey (https://www.magnetic-declination.com/Great%20Britain%20(UK)/Lee-on-the-Solent/)

pi_items.metadata_value: Fawley Chimney (~020T bearing) included as compass swing reference landmark
pi_items.verified_value: Fawley Power Station's 650-foot (198m) chimney was demolished at 7:00 AM on October 31, 2021 by controlled explosives and no longer exists
pi_items.source: News reports from October 2021 (https://www.itv.com/news/meridian/2021-10-29/650ft-chimney-at-fawley-power-station-set-to-be-demolished-this-weekend); multiple sources confirm demolition

pi_items.verified_value: MV Dorado is a real San Francisco Bay Ferry vessel (commissioned May 2022); the name is also used by multiple other commercial vessels
pi_items.source: San Francisco Bay Ferry website (https://sanfranciscobayferry.com/news/mv-dorado-celebration-press-release); MarineTraffic vessel database

pi_items.metadata_value: Calshot Spit Light as navigation reference (bearing ~040T)
pi_items.verified_value: Calshot Spit Light is a real navigation aid marking the Solent entrance; replaced lightship with light float (buoy) in mid-1960s
pi_items.source: MarineTraffic (https://www.marinetraffic.com/en/ais/details/lights/1000023762); historical records from National Historic Ships

pi_items.metadata_value: The Needles Lighthouse with bearing approximately 265°T
pi_items.verified_value: Needles Lighthouse is a real, active lighthouse on Isle of Wight; specific bearing data from Solent position not publicly verifiable
pi_items.source: Trinity House (https://www.trinityhouse.co.uk/lighthouses-and-lightvessels/needles-lighthouse); requires nautical chart consultation

pi_items.metadata_value: Hurst Point Lighthouse with bearing approximately 248°T
pi_items.verified_value: Hurst Point Lighthouse is a real, active lighthouse in Hampshire; specific bearing data from Solent position not publicly verifiable
pi_items.source: Trinity House (https://www.trinityhouse.co.uk/lighthouses-and-lightvessels/hurst-point-lighthouse); requires nautical chart consultation

pi_items.metadata_value: Scenario is a constructed training exercise in Bridge Command simulator
pi_items.verified_value: This is a synthetically generated task combining real Solent geography with fictional compass deviation coefficients for training purposes
pi_items.source: Analysis of task files and scenario construction

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic compass swing training scenario set in the real Solent maritime area using Bridge Command simulator. Verified facts: The Admiralty coefficient formula (Dev = A + B*sin(H) + C*cos(H) + D*sin(2H) + E*cos(2H)) and the provided coefficients (A=2.0, B=-3.0, C=1.0, D=-1.5, E=0.5) generate mathematically valid compass deviations ranging from -1.8° to +6.3° for the 24-point table (15-degree intervals). The Solent location coordinates (50.8050°N, 1.2850°W) are accurate. Bridge Command is a real open-source maritime simulator. SOLAS Chapter V does regulate magnetic compasses. However, there are significant issues: (1) The stated magnetic variation of ~1.0°W is contradicted by current World Magnetic Model data showing +0.68°E to +0.77°E (easterly, not westerly) for the Solent in 2024-2026; (2) The Fawley Power Station chimney, listed as a landmark reference (~020T), was demolished on October 31, 2021, and is no longer available as a navigation aid in 2026.
